title.ja: Claude Code のインストール

heading.ja: Claude Code のインストール

description.en: A beginner-friendly walkthrough for your very first Claude Code installation: install the CLI with one command, log in for the first time, and make Claude Code build your first tiny webpage. No prior Claude Code experience required.
description.ja: 初めて Claude Code をインストールする方向けの、初心者に優しいウォークスルー： ワンコマンドで CLI をインストールし、初回ログインを行い、 Claude Code に最初の小さなウェブページを作らせます。Claude Code の事前経験は不要です。

learnings.en: Installing Claude Code with a single curl command
learnings.ja: 単一の curl コマンドで Claude Code をインストールする
初回起動時にログインする

learnings.en: Running a first end-to-end prompt (build + open a webpage)
learnings.ja: 最初のエンドツーエンドのプロンプトを実行する（構築して開く）

steps.text:
┌  Claude Code v2.0
│  First launch — let's get you signed in.
└

comment.en: On the very first run, Claude Code detects it has no credentials and walks you through authentication before dropping you into a prompt.
comment.ja: 初回起動時、Claude Code は認証情報がないことを検出し、プロンプトに入る前に 認証フローを案内します。

steps.html:
How would you like to authenticate?

  1. Claude subscription (Pro / Max)
  2. API billing via Anthropic Console

comment.en: Pick option 1 if you already pay for Claude (Pro or Max) — it reuses your subscription quota. Option 2 is per-token billing via the Anthropic Console, useful for teams and heavier workloads.
comment.ja: すでに Claude（Pro または Max）を契約している場合は 1 を選びます — サブスクリプションのクォータをそのまま利用できます。2 は Anthropic Console 経由の トークン単位の課金で、チーム利用や負荷の高いワークロードに向いています。

steps.text:
Opening browser for sign-in…
✓ Authenticated as plunder.steffen.2a@kyoto-u.ac.jp

comment.en: A browser window pops open for OAuth. Sign in, approve the device, and the terminal picks the token back up automatically.
comment.ja: OAuth 用にブラウザウィンドウが開きます。サインインしてデバイスを承認すると、 ターミナル側が自動的にトークンを受け取ります。

round.prompt: Please create a small html page with a simulation of the game of life

steps.html: The directory is empty, so I'll create a fresh Game of Life page.

comment.en: Claude confirms the working directory is empty and plans to write a single self-contained HTML file — no build step, no dependencies.
comment.ja: Claude は作業ディレクトリが空であることを確認し、ビルドステップも依存関係もない 単一の自己完結型 HTML ファイルを作成する方針を立てます。

steps.code:
file: game-of-life.html
(~180 lines of HTML + inline CSS + JS)

comment.en: One Write call produces the whole page: canvas rendering, a toroidal grid, a speed slider, and Start / Random controls — all inline in one file.
comment.ja: 1 回の Write 呼び出しでページ全体を生成します：キャンバス描画、 トーラス状のグリッド、スピードスライダー、Start / Random コントロール — すべてが 1 ファイルにインライン化されています。

content.text:
Conway's Game of Life

…

steps.html: Done — game-of-life.html is ready. Open it in a browser, click Start, or Random to seed the whole board.

comment.en: Claude finishes with a short summary and a hint at the next action: opening the file to see it run.
comment.ja: Claude は短い要約と、次のアクション（ファイルを開いて動作を確認する）への ヒントを添えて締めくくります。

comment.en: On macOS, open hands the file to Launch Services, which routes .html to your default browser. It's the CLI equivalent of double-clicking in Finder.
comment.ja: macOS では open がファイルを Launch Services に渡し、 .html が既定のブラウザへルーティングされます。Finder で ダブルクリックするのと同等の CLI 操作です。

comment.en: The page loads straight from the filesystem over file:// — no server, no build. A glider drifts across the grid, and the controls let you seed a random board or tune the frame rate.
comment.ja: ページは file:// でファイルシステムから直接読み込まれます — サーバーもビルドも不要です。グライダーがグリッド上を進み、コントロールで ランダムな盤面の初期化やフレームレートの調整ができます。

steps.html: Opened in your default browser. Click Start to animate the glider, or Random to seed the whole board.

comment.en: That's the full loop: install, log in, prompt, result. From here, every Claude Code workflow is just another prompt in this same REPL.
comment.ja: これで一連のループが完成です：インストール、ログイン、プロンプト、結果。 ここから先の Claude Code のワークフローはすべて、この同じ REPL への 別のプロンプトにすぎません。

round.prompt: Please create a small html page with a simulation of the game of life

steps.text: The user wants a small HTML page with Conway's Game of Life. I'll first check what's in the working directory so I don't overwrite anything, then write a self-contained HTML file with canvas-based rendering, a toroidal grid, and simple controls.

steps.html:
The directory is empty, so I'll create a fresh Game of Life page.

★ Insight

  • Conway's Game of Life needs double buffering: we can't update cells in-place because each cell's next state depends on its neighbors' current state. Modifying live data mid-iteration corrupts the calculation.
  • Using a flat Uint8Array instead of a 2D array is much faster — contiguous memory, no nested array overhead, and the index y * cols + x maps directly to a CPU cache line.
  • We'll use requestAnimationFrame instead of setInterval — it syncs to the display refresh rate and pauses automatically in background tabs.

steps.code:
file_path: /Users/steffen/ping-pong/game-of-life.html
content: (5241 chars — full HTML document with canvas rendering,
         double-buffered Uint8Array grid, toroidal wrap, and
         Start/Step/Clear/Random/Speed controls)

content.text:
Conway's Game of Life

Generation: 0 · Alive: 0 · Click/drag to draw

steps.html:
★ Insight

  • Toroidal topology: (x + dx + cols) % cols wraps coordinates so the grid edges connect — a glider crossing the right edge reappears on the left. Without this, patterns "die" at the boundary.
  • Decoupling simulation from rendering: the loop uses requestAnimationFrame (ties to ~60fps screen refresh) but gates the actual step() behind a custom interval (1000/fps). This lets the speed slider control simulation rate independently of the render loop, and keeps the UI responsive.
  • Toggle-on-click drawing: on mousedown we read the cell's current state and invert it, then mousemove paints that same value. Start a drag on a dead cell and everything you touch becomes alive; start on a live cell and you erase.

Open game-of-life.html in a browser. A glider is pre-seeded in the corner — hit Start to watch it travel diagonally, or click Random to fill the board and observe emergent patterns (still lifes, oscillators, occasional gliders).

Opened in your default browser. The glider should be visible in the top-left — click Start to animate it, or Random to seed the whole board.

★ Insight

  • macOS's open command uses Launch Services to pick the default handler for the file type (.html → your default browser). It's the CLI equivalent of double-clicking in Finder.
  • Because the file is loaded via file:// rather than http://, there's no server involved — everything runs locally in the browser's JS engine. This also means some browser APIs (like fetch to relative paths, service workers, or SharedArrayBuffer) would be restricted under stricter CORS rules, but our self-contained page doesn't need them.
